_comment: Correct RCEReport extraction for the ALEKS Full Report PDF (Sample School District, 2025-26 BOY-MOY). Anchors format and value style. Header/text fields are high-confidence; effect-size and subgroup fields were read from the Outcome Analysis dot plot using the chart-reading protocol. Items that could not be read cleanly are kept as rows with null numerics + a notes flag, never dropped or guessed.
report_title: View Usage & Outcomes of ALEKS — Rapid Cycle Evaluation Summary for Sample School District
author: Amanda Cadran, PhD
analysis_method_notes: Descriptive statistics (means, SDs) for usage; partial correlations of total time on ALEKS with MOY Fastbridge aMath scores.
sample_size_notes: Outcomes analysis ran for Grade 6 only; not enough data for other grades.

usage.usage_compliance_threshold: 665 Minutes on system (≈30 minutes per week)
usage.usage_notes: 97% of students used ALEKS; average 486 minutes on system over 25 weeks. 156 students (27%) met or exceeded the recommended dosage.

subgroup_findings.subgroup_value: Asian / Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander / Two or more races
subgroup_findings.notes: Visual Ambiguity: dots present on the Ethnicity effect-size plot but callouts not legible / leader lines unresolved. Flagged for human spot-check rather than guessed.

summary_key_findings: Grade 6 students with more time on ALEKS had higher MOY Fastbridge aMath scores (effect size +0.20, n=572). Results are statistically significant and meet ESSA Tier III (Promising Evidence). Only 27% of students met the recommended dosage despite 97% using the product — a low-usage/high-impact pattern.
next_steps_verbatim: Discuss results with the district team (Appendix C). Consider additional EOY RCEs using updated 2025-2026 usage and outcomes data.
